title: Extreme Ownership: How U.S. Navy SEALs Lead and Win
author: Jocko Willink
description: Retired Navy SEAL commanders Jocko Willink and Leif Babin translate the brutal lessons of combat leadership in Iraq's Anbar Province into a practical leadership framework for business and life. Each chapter pairs a vivid combat story with its corresponding leadership principle: extreme ownership (no excuses, ever), cover and move (team cohesion), simple (clear plans that people understand), prioritize and execute (triage the chaos), and decentralized command (give your team the authority to lead). The core thesis: there are no bad teams, only bad leaders — and the moment you stop blaming external forces, you become dangerous as a leader.
